tossups.question: An elaborate cipher system in this novel uses the book The Sins of the Father but is rendered useless when Biegler points out that the decipherers have the wrong edition of the novel. In this novel, the gluttonous Baroun is punished by being restrained near a kitchen so he constantly smells food he cannot eat. A doctor in this novel alternates “enema and aspirin” and “stomach pump and quinine” to deal with patients suspected of malingering. The (*) Glorious Licking Continues is the last section of this unfinished novel, whose protagonist pulls a train’s emergency brake and is arrested for saying that flies defecate on the emperor’s portrait. Its protagonist serves as the assistant to an alcoholic Catholic chaplain before being lost to Lieutenant Lukas in a poker game. For 10 points, an incompetent dogcatcher is drafted into World War I in what satirical novel by Jaroslav Hasek?
tossups.question_sanitized: An elaborate cipher system in this novel uses the book The Sins of the Father but is rendered useless when Biegler points out that the decipherers have the wrong edition of the novel. In this novel, the gluttonous Baroun is punished by being restrained near a kitchen so he constantly smells food he cannot eat. A doctor in this novel alternates "enema and aspirin" and "stomach pump and quinine" to deal with patients suspected of malingering. The (*) Glorious Licking Continues is the last section of this unfinished novel, whose protagonist pulls a train's emergency brake and is arrested for saying that flies defecate on the emperor's portrait. Its protagonist serves as the assistant to an alcoholic Catholic chaplain before being lost to Lieutenant Lukas in a poker game. For 10 points, an incompetent dogcatcher is drafted into World War I in what satirical novel by Jaroslav Hasek?
tossups.answer: The Fate of the Good Soldier Švejk during the World War [or Osudy dobrého vojáka Švejka za světové válkyi] (Švejk is pronounced “sch-VAYK,” but accept any reasonable attempt at pronunciation.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: The Fate of the Good Soldier Svejk during the World War [or Osudy dobreho vojaka Svejka za svetove valkyi] (Svejk is pronounced "sch-VAYK," but accept any reasonable attempt at pronunciation.)

tossups.question: This novel’s narrator describes stepping into a “prison full of screaming hysterics” rather than into a minuet. The narrator becomes aware of something “treacherous” and “evil” in the day after observing one character laying a finger upon the wrist of another character while at Martin Luther’s Protest. A character in this novel who has an affair with an artist named Jimmy commits suicide by (*) drinking prussic acid. A telegram that says “Safe Brindisi. Have a rattling good time” is sent by a character in this novel who repeats the word “shuttlecocks.” The line “this is the saddest story I have ever heard” opens this novel, in which Florence Dowell has an affair with Edward Ashburnham. For 10 points, name this novel by Ford Madox Ford.

Have a rattling good time" is sent by a character in this novel who repeats the word "shuttlecocks." The line "this is the saddest story I have ever heard" opens this novel, in which Florence Dowell has an affair with Edward Ashburnham. For 10 points, name this novel by Ford Madox Ford.

tossups.question: In this novel, a casino orchestra’s rendition of the Rákóczi (“RAH-koh-tsee”) March drowns out the footsteps of an approaching woman, allowing her to overhear a man’s confession of love to his ward. A woman in this novel first makes the acquaintance of Maisie Maidan after helping untangle a comb from her hair. Upon hearing of the title character’s suicide en route to Ceylon, a girl in this novel becomes catatonic and can only say a prayer to an “Omnipotent God” and the word “shuttlecock.” Characters in this novel who seek treatment for their heart ailments at a spa in Nauheim include the narrator’s promiscuous wife Florence. This novel opens with the line “This is the saddest story I have ever heard.” For 10 points, name this novel by Ford Madox Ford titled for Edward Ashburnham, a British army officer.

Upon hearing of the title character's suicide en route to Ceylon, a girl in this novel becomes catatonic and can only say a prayer to an "Omnipotent God" and the word "shuttlecock." Characters in this novel who seek treatment for their heart ailments at a spa in Nauheim include the narrator's promiscuous wife Florence. This novel opens with the line "This is the saddest story I have ever heard." For 10 points, name this novel by Ford Madox Ford titled for Edward Ashburnham, a British army officer.

tossups.question: To crack a cipher, a character with this job is tasked with buying Volume II of Ludwig Ganghofer's novel The Sins of the Father. A character with this job smokes a white pipe and drinks with the occultist cook Jurajda and the perpetually hungry batman Baloun in illustrations by Josef Lada. A protagonist with this job receives a "certificate of imbecility" and is a former dogcatcher who unknowingly steals a superior's dog and gifts it to Lukáš. Jaroslav Hašek created a "Good" character in this profession named Švejk (sh-VAKE). A man is haunted by his murder of the printer Gerard Duval after the schoolteacher Kantorek convinces him to join this profession. For 10 points, name this profession of Paul Baumer in All Quiet on the Western Front.

For 10 points, name this profession of Paul Baumer in All Quiet on the Western Front.

tossups.question: A man with this profession who repeats “Humbly report” refuses to be “superarbitrated” and takes an instruction to go to hell literally. Another man with this profession, Chris, does not remember his past 15 years and believes that he is still 20, causing him to be in love with the middle-aged Margaret; that occurs in Rebecca West’s novel about “The Return of” one of these people. Another of these people accidentally hits a train’s emergency brake, is lost in a poker game by the alcoholic minister Katz, and works for Lukáš. (*) Švejk (“sh’VAKE”), that “Good” one of these people, titles a novel by Jaroslav Hašek (“YAH-roh-slav HAH-shek”). Another boy is manipulated into joining this profession by his teacher Kantorek; that friend of Albert Kropp receives Kemmerich’s boots after his death. For 10 points, name this profession of Paul Baumer in Erich Maria Remarque’s All Quiet on the Western Front.
tossups.answer: soldier [accept The Good Soldier Švejk or The Return of the Soldier; prompt on more specific ranks or descriptions]

tossups.question: One character in this novel is found in Ceylon only able to say “Credo in unum Deum omnipotentem” and “shuttle-cock”. The title character tries to comfort a young girl on a train in an episode known as the Kilsyte case. The wife of the narrator commits suicide with (+) prussic acid from a vial she had told her husband was full of heart medicine. The title character visits Luther’s protest while staying with his wife at (*) Nauheim; he was there while letting his house at Branshaw Teleragh to recover his losses from Monte Carlo, including a costly affair with La Dolciquita. This novel opens “This is the saddest story I have ever heard” and ends with the title character’s suicide when his mistress, Nancy Rufford, leaves for India. For ten points, name this novel about Edward Ashburnham written by Ford Madox Ford.
tossups.answer: The Good Soldier: A Tale of Passion

tossups.question: During a psychiatric examination, this character is asked whether radium is heavier than lead and responds that he has not weighed them. For one assignment, this character only packs copies of the first part of a novel, although its second part was being used to decipher telegrams. After this man loses his documents, he begins walking west instead of south to the front at České Budějovice (CHESS-keh BOO-jeh-YO-vit-seh). At the beginning of the novel this character appears in, he sells mongrel dogs before being arrested for insulting Emperor Franz Josef I. This character is eventually captured by his own forces when he tries on a Russian uniform. For 10 points, name this "feeble-minded" Czech, the title "Good Soldier" of a Jaroslav Hašek (YAH-roh-slav HAH-shek) novel.
tossups.answer: Josef Švejk (YO-sef sh-VAKE) [or The Good Soldier Švejk; or Josef Švejk]

tossups.question: While looking at a beautiful insane girl, this character compares her appearance to a “picture without meaning.” In a comic interlude, this character returns to Philadelphia to argue over an inheritance with the elderly, indistinguishable Hurlbird sisters. In a key scene, this character encounters his former acquaintance Mr. Bagshawe, who tells him that his wife cuckolded him with a painter named Jimmy. During the first section of the novel he appears in, he narrates an excursion to (*) Martin Luther’s apartments at Marburg from the health spa of Nauheim. He ends up buying Branshaw Manor from Leonora and taking care of the near-catatonic Nancy Rufford. He declares “This is the saddest story I have ever heard” at the beginning of a novel about his wife Florence’s affair with Edward Ashburnham. For 10 points, name this narrator of Ford Madox Ford’s novel The Good Soldier.
tossups.answer: John Dowell [accept either name] [do not accept or prompt on “The Good Soldier”]

tossups.question: One character in this novel known for giving long speeches explaining mundane objects is run over by a cart while giving an impromptu talk on the defnition of pavement. The author gives a self-portrait in a character who edits the magazine Animal World before being fred for inventing fctional beasts. In a chapter named for the protagonist’s “Anabasis,” he wanders the countryside afer being kicked of a train for accidentally hitting the emergency brake. The protagonist works for the alcoholic chaplain Otto Katz, who substitutes a sports trophy for the communion chalice and loses him in a poker game to Lukas. The main character of this novel uses his experience as a dogcatcher to steal Colonel Kraus von Zillergut’s dog as a gif for his com-manding ofcer, who does not know the dog is stolen. This novel’s title character is initially put in an asylum as unft to serve, but goes to the front afer his cleaning woman takes him to a recruiting ofce in a wheelchair. At the end, the title character is arrested by his own troops afer trying on a Russian soldier’s uniform. For 10 points, name this picaresque novel about a foolish Czech infantryman by Jaroslav Hasek.
tossups.answer: The <u>Good Soldier Svejk</u> [accept <u>Osudy Dobreho Vojaka Svejka za Svetove Valky</u>]

bonuses.leadin: This movement’s manifesto listed things to be “blasted,” such as England’s climate “for its sins and infections,” and things to be “blessed,” like hairdressers and mariners, since they both fight nature. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this British literary and artistic movement, whose manifesto was published by Wyndham Lewis and published in the magazine BLAST.

The first edition of BLAST published a short story that would grow into this novel, which begins “This is the saddest story I have ever heard.” It ends with the narrator passively letting Edward cut his throat with a pen-knife.
This man’s wife, Dorothy Shakespear, published some paintings in BLAST. He named the movement of vorticism as well as using its influence in The Cantos.
answers: Ezra Pound (Yes, Ezra Pound’s wife’s maiden name really was “Shakespear.” Her mother, Olivia Shakespear, was also a novelist and had an affair with Yeats.)

bonuses.leadin: In this novel, Florence commits suicide by taking prussic acid because she assumes that her husband has discovered her affair with Edward Ashburnham. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Ford Madox Ford novel that begins with the line “This is the saddest story I have ever heard.”
The main character of this novel, by a different author, commits suicide by swallowing prussic acid after the financial ruin of a railroad project between Salt Lake City and Veracruz.
answers: The Way We Live Now [by Anthony Trollope]
answers_sanitized: The Way We Live Now [by Anthony Trollope]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In this novel, Florence commits suicide by taking prussic acid because she assumes that her husband has discovered her affair with Edward Ashburnham. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this Ford Madox Ford novel that begins with the line "This is the saddest story I have ever heard."
The main character of this novel, by a different author, commits suicide by swallowing prussic acid after the financial ruin of a railroad project between Salt Lake City and Veracruz.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about soldiers in European literature, for 10 points each.
parts: This German poet wrote several Letters to a Young Poet to a military cadet at his alma mater. This author’s poetry collections include the Sonnets to Orpheus.

This soldier’s misadventures include trekking around Bohemia looking for his barracks and being taken as a POW by his own side. This comic character’s name came to be a verb for sabotaging one’s superiors with stupidity.

Ford Madox Ford’s Parade’s End novels are about Christopher Tietjens, who serves as a soldier in this war. It is also the setting of Erich Maria Remarque’s The Road Back and All Quiet on the Western Front.
answers: Svejk [SH’VAYK] [accept The Good Soldier Svejk, written by Jaroslav Hašek]
World War I [or the First World War; or the Great War]
answers_sanitized: Svejk [SH'VAYK] [accept The Good Soldier Svejk, written by Jaroslav Hasek]
World War I [or the First World War; or the Great War]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about soldiers in European literature, for 10 points each.
parts_sanitized: This German poet wrote several Letters to a Young Poet to a military cadet at his alma mater. This author's poetry collections include the Sonnets to Orpheus.

This soldier's misadventures include trekking around Bohemia looking for his barracks and being taken as a POW by his own side. This comic character's name came to be a verb for sabotaging one's superiors with stupidity.

Ford Madox Ford's Parade's End novels are about Christopher Tietjens, who serves as a soldier in this war. It is also the setting of Erich Maria Remarque's The Road Back and All Quiet on the Western Front.

bonuses.leadin: This novel’s dog-stealing hero sells fake pedigrees and faces many enemas while in the sanatorium of a garrison prison. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel whose hero undergoes an eventful “anabasis” after losing touch with his regiment en route to Galicia.

In a train prison van, Švejk meets the volunteer Marek. While editor of Animal World, Marek was accused of introducing Germanised words for birds that diluted the purity of this language, in which The Good Soldier Švejk was written.

Švejk’s first job in the army is as batman to Otto Katz, who gets drunk on communion wine while serving in this role. Yossarian loved another character with this profession “at first sight” in the opening of Catch-22.
answers: The Good Soldier Švejk [SHVAYK] [or Osudy dobrého vojáka Švejka za světové války]
answers_sanitized: The Good Soldier Svejk [SHVAYK] [or Osudy dobreho vojaka Svejka za svetove valky]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This novel's dog-stealing hero sells fake pedigrees and faces many enemas while in the sanatorium of a garrison prison. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this novel whose hero undergoes an eventful "anabasis" after losing touch with his regiment en route to Galicia.

In a train prison van, Svejk meets the volunteer Marek. While editor of Animal World, Marek was accused of introducing Germanised words for birds that diluted the purity of this language, in which The Good Soldier Svejk was written.

Svejk's first job in the army is as batman to Otto Katz, who gets drunk on communion wine while serving in this role. Yossarian loved another character with this profession "at first sight" in the opening of Catch-22.

bonuses.leadin: Cecil Parrott included a map in his translation of a chapter charting the "Anabasis" of the title character of this novel, who abets the chaplain Otto Katz's love of booze. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this darkly comic novel about a dog trader roped into fighting in the First World War, which was written by Jaroslav Hasek.
This other World War I novel concerns the enlistment of Paul Baumer and his schoolmates. It was written by Erich Maria Remarque.

A Russian perspective on the war is provided by this author's novel August 1914. He is better known for a novel in which the radiotherapist Vera Gangart and the nurse Zoya stave off the advances of the infirm Oleg Kostoglotov.
answers: The Good Soldier Svejk [or The Good Soldier Schweik; or The Fateful Adventures of the Good Soldier Svejk During the World War; or Osudy dobreho vojaka Svejka za svetove valky]
answers_sanitized: The Good Soldier Svejk [or The Good Soldier Schweik; or The Fateful Adventures of the Good Soldier Svejk During the World War; or Osudy dobreho vojaka Svejka za svetove valky]
All Quiet on the Western Front

parts_sanitized: Name this darkly comic novel about a dog trader roped into fighting in the First World War, which was written by Jaroslav Hasek.
This other World War I novel concerns the enlistment of Paul Baumer and his schoolmates. It was written by Erich Maria Remarque.

A Russian perspective on the war is provided by this author's novel August 1914. He is better known for a novel in which the radiotherapist Vera Gangart and the nurse Zoya stave off the advances of the infirm Oleg Kostoglotov.

bonuses.leadin: The character of Lieutenant Dub in this novel was based on its author’s experiences in the 91st Infantry Regiment with Lieutenant Mechalek. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel by Jaroslav Hašek about a “certified imbecile” who serves in the Austro–Hungarian Empire.
Hašek was a writer of this ethnicity, shared by the author of The Unbearable Lightness of Being, Milan Kundera.
Kundera collected stories like “Dr. Havel After Twenty Years” and “Eduard and God” into this short story collection.
answers: The Fateful Adventures of the Good Soldier Švejk During the World War (accept Osudy Dobrého Vojáka Švejka za Světové Války; accept Good Soldier Schweik or Good Soldier Schwejk)
answers_sanitized: The Fateful Adventures of the Good Soldier Svejk During the World War (accept Osudy Dobreho Vojaka Svejka za Svetove Valky; accept Good Soldier Schweik or Good Soldier Schwejk)
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The character of Lieutenant Dub in this novel was based on its author's experiences in the 91st Infantry Regiment with Lieutenant Mechalek. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this novel by Jaroslav Hasek about a "certified imbecile" who serves in the Austro-Hungarian Empire.
Hasek was a writer of this ethnicity, shared by the author of The Unbearable Lightness of Being, Milan Kundera.
Kundera collected stories like "Dr. Havel After Twenty Years" and "Eduard and God" into this short story collection.

bonuses.leadin: This novel ends with John Yossarian nearly being killed by Nately’s whore. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this war satire by Joseph Heller, set on the island of Pianosa.
Joseph Heller was inspired by this novel about a seemingly incompetent soldier during World War I. It is the best known work of Czech author Jaroslav Hašek.
In The Good Soldier Švejk, the title character lives his civilian life before the war as a dealer in these animals. Another character, Bretschneider, is eaten by these animals.
answers: The Fateful Adventures of the Good Soldier Švejk During the World War [or Osudy dobrého vojáka Švejka za světové války]

packet.name: MIT A - Illinois B - TAMU A

answers_sanitized: The Fateful Adventures of the Good Soldier Svejk During the World War [or Osudy dobreho vojaka Svejka za svetove valky]

bonuses.leadin: This novel opens with the line “This is the saddest story I have ever heard”. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel about the philandering Captain Edward Ashburnham, narrated by John Dowell, whose wife Florence pretends to have a heart ailment
The Good Soldier was written by this English author of the Parade’s End tetralogy.
Another literary British soldier, Robbie Turner, is sent to prison when Briony Tallis falsely accuses him of rape in this 2001 novel.
